headline: Broadway Breaks the Fourth Wall: Isa Briones Calls Out Overzealous Fans
lede: Stage veteran Isa Briones is drawing a sharp line between her television persona and theatrical performance after recent audience disruptions during her Broadway run.
body:
Broadway's latest jukebox musical, *Just in Time*, is experiencing unexpected dramatic moments — and not from the script. Actress Isa Briones, fresh off her run on HBO Max's *The Pitt*, has confronted an emerging trend of audience members blurring the boundaries between character and performer.

Briones, currently portraying legendary singer Connie Francis, has publicly challenged theatergoers who seem intent on treating live performance like an interactive experience. Her stern social media warning signals a growing tension between performer expectations and audience engagement in contemporary theater.

The musical, which chronicles Francis's tumultuous musical journey, requires nuanced dramatic interpretation. Briones's frustration stems from audience members who apparently shout references to her previous television role, seemingly unable to distinguish between her character Dr. Trinity Santos and her current stage persona.

This incident highlights a broader conversation about audience etiquette in live performance spaces. Theater professionals have long battled the challenge of maintaining performance integrity while keeping productions accessible and engaging.

Briones's candid remarks suggest a generational shift in performance expectations, where fourth-wall conventions are increasingly challenged by audiences accustomed to more interactive entertainment mediums.
key_facts: Briones stars in Broadway's *Just in Time*
Performer called out disruptive audience behavior
Musical depicts Connie Francis's life story
Incident reflects changing theater audience dynamics
